name: St Austell Riverside Loop
terrain: Flat riverside path with tarmac and compacted gravel.
highlights: River views, café stops, bench seating, easy footing.
accessibility: Level route with gentle ramps and frequent seats.
safetyNotes: Keep to the riverside path and cross only at the marked footbridge.
busInfo: Bus 21 stops 150m from the start point at Riverfront.
itinerary: Start at the car park, follow the river east to Castle Grounds, return via the pedestrian bridge and town centre.

name: Fowey Harbour Calm Coast
terrain: Paved towpath and harbour walk, mostly flat.
highlights: Harbour views, cafes, accessible town centre, water-based wildlife.
accessibility: Wide towpath sections with a few gentle slopes.
safetyNotes: Watch for cyclists on the waterfront path and uneven paving at the quayside.
busInfo: Stagecoach route 95 stops at Fowey Town.
itinerary: From the quay, walk east along the harbour, cross the ferry lane, return via the quiet riverside path.

name: Truro Cathedral Garden Circuit
terrain: Paved city paths, park lawns and low steps.
highlights: Cathedral views, river garden, sheltered city park.
accessibility: Mostly paved, with some narrow city pavement sections.
safetyNotes: Use pedestrian crossings in busy city centre sections.
busInfo: Truro bus station has frequent connections from across Cornwall.
itinerary: Start at the bus station, head through Boscawen Park, pass the cathedral, return via Lemon Quay.

name: Newquay Headland Breeze
terrain: Coastal cliff path with packed sand and compacted trail.
highlights: Coastline views, beach cafes, iconic Newquay headland.
accessibility: Some uneven sections and short steep ramps.
safetyNotes: Stay well back from cliff edges and follow waymarked coastal path.
busInfo: Buses stop on Fore Street, 10 minutes from the start.
itinerary: Walk the cliff tops from Lusty Glaze to Towan Head and finish at Fistral Beach.

name: Perranporth Beach Boardwalk
terrain: Beach promenade, boardwalk and level steps.
highlights: Sea views, beach cafes, dunes and coastal breeze.
accessibility: Promenade sections with accessible café terraces.
safetyNotes: Avoid the shoreline during very high tide; keep clear of surf launches.
busInfo: Local buses arrive at Perranporth village centre.
itinerary: Enjoy the promenade east, return along the beach at low tide and stop at the café.

name: Looe Valley Timber Trail
terrain: Forest path with tree roots and compact soil.
highlights: River valley trees, quiet woodland, historic viaduct.
accessibility: Forest terrain may be uneven but low gradient.
safetyNotes: May be muddy after rain. Wear sturdy shoes.
busInfo: Trains and buses serve Looe station; walk begins nearby.
itinerary: Follow the valley trail to the viaduct, then loop back through riverside woodland.

name: Padstow Camel Creek
terrain: Coastal footpath and harbour walkway with flat sections.
highlights: Estuary views, coastal villages, ice cream stops.
accessibility: Mostly level with a short slope at the estuary crossing.
safetyNotes: Take care near the quay and follow marked paths through the village.
busInfo: Padstow bus stop is 5 minutes from the start.
itinerary: Cross the estuary footbridge, walk the Camel Creek path and return via the harbour front.

name: Helston Forest Friends
terrain: Mixed forest tracks, grass trails and stone steps.
highlights: Ancient woodland, picnic glades, community park.
accessibility: Some uneven sections; not recommended for wheelchair users.
safetyNotes: Watch for root hazards and damp leaves after rain.
busInfo: Local bus services run to Helston town centre; taxi recommended for the forest car park.
itinerary: Circle through the forest clearings, pass the pond, finish at the picnic meadow.

name: Bodmin Moor Pine Ridge
terrain: Gravel tracks, moorland footpaths and rocky sections.
highlights: Moorland skyline, forest views, historic stone bridges.
accessibility: Not suitable for wheelchairs or those needing firm level paths.
safetyNotes: Route crosses exposed moorland; bring layers and water.
busInfo: Bus 555 runs to Bodmin and allows connection to nearby routes.
itinerary: Head through the woods, climb Pine Ridge, return through the lower moorland trail.

name: St Ives Bay Path
accessibility: Paved harbour sections; cliff path includes steps.
safetyNotes: Steep steps at some points; remain on marked path.
busInfo: St Ives train and bus stations are a 5-minute walk from the start.
itinerary: Follow the harbour front, climb to the coastal path and finish at Porthminster.

name: Penzance Promenade Walk
terrain: Promenade pavement and level coastal paths.
accessibility: Level promenade throughout with plenty of seating.
safetyNotes: Keep a safe distance from the seawall in windy weather.
busInfo: Regular buses stop at the railway station outside the start point.
itinerary: Walk the seafront east to Jubilee Pool and return along the Inlet.

name: Polperro Village Circuit
terrain: Cobblestone streets and coastal paths with some steps.
highlights: Historic fishing village, harbour views, tea rooms.
accessibility: Cobblestones and steps make parts of this route challenging.
safetyNotes: Use handrails in steep village lanes and watch for touring traffic.
busInfo: Local coach drop-off near the village centre.
itinerary: Explore the harbour, climb to the village green, return through the old lane.

name: Gwithian Sands Shoreline
terrain: Firm sand shore and flat beach promenade.
highlights: Sea air, dunes, coastal café stops.
accessibility: Beach access can be soft sand; promenade sections easier.
safetyNotes: Tide-aware route; check tide times before walking the shoreline.
busInfo: Summer buses run to the beach car park.
itinerary: Walk east along the sand, return on the paved promenade.

name: Mousehole Harbour Peace
terrain: Village lanes and harbour path with gentle slopes.
accessibility: Some sloping cobbles but the harbour paths are level.
safetyNotes: Harbour edge is uneven; keep close to the quay wall.
busInfo: Local bus service stops 10 minutes from the village.
itinerary: Follow the harbour edge, pass the boats and return through the village lanes.
